summary: Iteration 15 — Forgot-password + Reset-password + Delete-account + Emergent-branding-removal features. BACKEND: 12/12 new tests in test_forgot_password_and_delete.py PASS (forgot generic-200 on unknown email, forgot creates HMAC-SHA256 hashed token doc, 60s cooldown does not rotate, reset 200 + old-pwd-401 + new-pwd-200, invalid token 400 code='invalid_or_expired_token', single-use second call 400, short password 422, DELETE /auth/me wrong-pwd 403 code='invalid_password', correct-pwd 200 with deleted_entries/deleted_cycles + Mongo purge of users+entries+cycles+email_verification_tokens+password_reset_tokens, old JWT 401, email re-registration succeeds with email_verified=false, unauthenticated 401). REGRESSION: 70 passed + 1 skipped across test_email_verification, test_email_verification_extra, test_leave_cycle, test_leave_reconciliation, test_cycle_max_days_cap, test_rest_gap_contract — full regression intact. FRONTEND: Login forgot-password link, /forgot-password page + forgot-sent-message, /reset-password (no-token error + valid-token success → reset-goto-login), login with new pwd → /dashboard → account-link → /account → email shown → delete-confirm-modal → wrong-pwd shows 'Mot de passe incorrect.' inline error → correct pwd deletes & redirects to /login. EMERGENT BRANDING removed: #emergent-badge count=0 and 'Made with Emergent' text=false on /login, /forgot-password, /verify-email, /dashboard, /account; a[href*='emergent.sh'] count=0 on all those pages. Admin (admin@routier-facile.fr / Admin123!) login still returns 200.

critical_code_review_comments: Security model for password reset is solid: HMAC-SHA256 hash at rest (verified via Mongo — token_hash != raw token observed in backend log), 1h TTL via Mongo expireAfterSeconds=0 (implicit via TTL on expires_at field — covered by single-use + invalid-after-consume tests), single-use (doc deleted on consume), 60s cooldown (token_hash unchanged on rapid repeat), and generic 'Si un compte existe' response prevents email enumeration.

DELETE /auth/me uses request body with JSON {password} and returns purge counts — clean implementation. Verified that ALL related collections (users, entries, cycles, email_verification_tokens, password_reset_tokens) are wiped, and that the JWT becomes unusable while the email can be re-registered (email_verified=false reset).
Emergent branding removal is complete and defensively guarded by CSS (#emergent-badge and a[href*='emergent.sh'] display:none in index.css). Confirmed 0 occurrences across all 5 audited routes.

SMTP no-op fallback logs the reset URL with the RAW token at WARNING level — fine for preview/CI but ensure production logs (Render) filter/redact this to avoid token leakage via log aggregation. Same concern was flagged for verify-email tokens in iter14 — still applies.

server.py file size keeps growing (now includes new helpers create_password_reset_token/consume_password_reset_token/send_password_reset_email + 3 new endpoints around line ~700). Modularising into routers/services is overdue; flagged in iters 13–14 still applicable.

ResetPassword.jsx unlike VerifyEmail.jsx does NOT have the same React.StrictMode side-effect-on-mount issue because the POST is gated behind explicit form submit (user action) — verified by reset flow succeeding end-to-end in the browser.
Account.jsx delete confirmation modal correctly clears state, redirects to /login, and clears auth cookie/localStorage — verified by post-delete /login landing with no auth state.

success_rate.backend: 100% — 12/12 new + 70/70 regression PASS (1 pre-existing skip in test_leave_cycle)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 10 audited E2E flows PASS, no Emergent branding detected

test_credentials: Admin: admin@routier-facile.fr / Admin123! (verified still working). Per-test fresh users registered via /api/auth/register + email_verified flipped in Mongo by conftest.register_verified_user helper.
seed_data_creation: None persistent — every test creates and deletes its own user. Backend tests use TEST_/test_ prefixes; frontend tests use test_fe_fp_/test_fe_del_ prefixes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 features (forgot/reset password, delete account, Emergent branding removal) are FULLY VERIFIED end-to-end (backend pytest + frontend Playwright). The previous iteration's VerifyEmail.jsx StrictMode double-fire bug is OUT OF SCOPE for this iteration; if main agent fixed it separately, it can be re-tested by tailing backend.err.log for a fresh verify-email?token URL and opening it. To regenerate a reset token for manual testing: `tail -n 200 /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log | grep -o 'reset-password?token=[^ ]*'` after a /api/auth/forgot-password POST. The delete endpoint expects DELETE with a JSON body — use `requests.request('DELETE', url, json={'password': ...})` (verified working in test_forgot_password_and_delete.py::TestDeleteAccount).
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found in this iteration. The transient 'login_ok timeout' observed in the Playwright run was a wait_for_url matcher false-negative (page actually navigated to /dashboard and rendered correctly with the user's FE greeting and account-link button visible in screenshot); all subsequent dependent assertions on /dashboard and /account succeeded, confirming login completed normally.
